title: Tuples
verdict: PASS - reading.md meets length, citation, forward-reference, faithfulness, tone, and project format requirements.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# Tuples' H1; no YAML frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json parses with word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections. Sections present in exact order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length.notes: Prose word count (code fences excluded) sits mid-range at ~1365. Minute figure in sidecar (7) is slightly higher than words/230 (~5.9) but the word budget is the gate and is satisfied.

citations.notes: [1] Real Python lists-tuples, [2] W3Schools python_tuples, [3] docs.python.org datastructures each cited inline (e.g. [1] L5, [2] L11, [3] L20). Bibliography L150-152 has exactly three entries matching inline markers. No orphan markers, no orphan entries, no fabricated sources.

forward_references.notes: Prior P1/P2 and 4.1 Lists vocabulary used freely and correctly (tuples taught by contrast with lists, as intended). No later topics taught as known: no sets/dicts (week 5), no iterators/generators/Counter/defaultdict (week 5), no named tuples/dataclasses (later). The one dict-key aside at L50 ('you will later be able to use one as a dictionary key where a list cannot') is a permitted forward-awareness note - it does not teach dictionary syntax or operations.

faithfulness.notes: Sampled assertions all trace to corpus: ordered/immutable sequence (5.1); comma makes a tuple and (42,) gotcha (5.1); tuple() takes one iterable, tuple(1,2,3) TypeError (5.1); exactly two methods count()/index() (5.3, 5.7); index() raises ValueError, guard with in (5.7); lexicographic comparison and shorter-is-less rule (5.8); variable swap RHS evaluated before assignment (5.4); star-unpacking yields a list, at most one star (5.4); chained indexing matrix[0][2] (5.5); worked example records/sorted/rebind and mutable-list-of-fixed-tuples framing (section 6).

tone.notes: Plainspoken, second-person, beginner-friendly. Average sentence length under 25 words; no academic hedging phrases. embeds_diagram is false in sidecar and no image is embedded or required (diagram=false).

format_check_project.notes: Every Key Concepts sub-topic leads with an underlined bold label <u>**Label.**</u> (L11, L20, L32, L43, L52, L64, L74, L85, L95). Run-together enumerations are rendered as Markdown bullet lists rather than buried in prose: Operations +/*/in bulleted (L76-78), count()/index() methods bulleted (L82-83), and the tuple-vs-list decision procedure bulleted (L97-101).
